raw:
{
  "judge_id": "...",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.1", "R1.2", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.1": 5,
    "R1.2": 3,
    "RX.1": 3,
    "RX.3": 3,
    "RX.4": 5,
    "RX.5": 3
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The agent correctly identifies the conceptual issue with the paper's framing and proposes a reframing that survives prior-work contrast.",
    "R1.2": "The agent partially supports its claim of logical consistency, but does not engage the argument chain end-to-end.",
    "RX.1": "The agent flags uncertainty for one clearly out-of-reach claim (the 2×2 result table), but does not mark confidence and missing evidence for each recommendation.",
    "RX.3": "The output covers mostly within scope, with one drift in the discussion of related work.",
    "RX.4": "The output is harm-free, but introduces minor regressions in the discussion of evaluation choices.",
    "RX.5": "The agent annotates some non-cosmetic changes with source and reason, but not all."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.1": 0.9,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "RX.1": 0.8,
    "RX.3": 0.6,
    "RX.4": 0.9,
    "RX.5": 0.8
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.1: The agent correctly identifies the conceptual issue with the paper's framing and proposes a reframing that survives prior-work contrast.
rationale.R1.2: The agent partially supports its claim of logical consistency, but does not engage the argument chain end-to-end.
rationale.RX.1: The agent flags uncertainty for one clearly out-of-reach claim (the 2×2 result table), but does not mark confidence and missing evidence for each recommendation.
rationale.RX.3: The output covers mostly within scope, with one drift in the discussion of related work.
rationale.RX.4: The output is harm-free, but introduces minor regressions in the discussion of evaluation choices.
rationale.RX.5: The agent annotates some non-cosmetic changes with source and reason, but not all.
